results.title: sisyphus-escalator-v01
results.prompt: Use the attached reference image for STYLE ONLY — borrow its litho-crayon line quality, its tri-tone palette, its paper grain and ink-starvation texture, and its flat printmaking technique. Do NOT borrow its subject (the watching figure, the crowd, the dog-head poster), its composition, or any scene element from it. Render a completely new scene in that style: Sisyphus as a stooped commuter shoving a heavy stalled shopping cart up a long broken-down escalator in a derelict transit hall, head bowed, coat heavy on his shoulders. The escalator climbs into shadow above him. Lonely, weary, monumental.

resolved_params.system_prompt: A early-20th-century Die Brücke Expressionist multi-color stone lithograph in the spirit of Munch, Kirchner, and Nolde. Forms drawn with a thick, blunt, greasy litho-crayon line that drags and crumbles on coarse textured stone — no fine detail, no clean vector edges, no anti-aliasing. Flat stencil-like color blocks printed behind a dark key-line layer, with slightly offset registration that leaves unprinted warm-cream paper borders. Coarse organic paper tooth throughout; imperfect, hand-rolled ink coverage with visible ink starvation — speckled white paper grain breaking through the dark ink. Restricted low-saturation tri-tone palette: warm cream paper, dusty rose-pink, and deep Prussian blue, with at most a single muted terracotta-red accent. Stark, flat, high-contrast graphic shapes; form defined by flat silhouettes and heavy outlines. Absolutely no gradients, no digital glow, no volumetric shading, no specular highlights, no photorealism, and never a sterile pure-white background — the substrate is always warm fibrous aged-cream paper.
